fresh_facts.claim: UAE Defense Ministry reported engaging 56 Iranian drones and 23 ballistic missiles over a 24-hour period, totaling 79 attacks.
Jordan's military stated Iran targeted the kingdom with missiles and drones in the past 24 hours, including at least one missile and one drone.

Pakistan's army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir was in contact with US Vice President JD Vance, special envoy Steve Witkoff, and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi regarding the 'Islamabad Accords' ceasefire proposal.
The proposed 'Islamabad Accords' include an immediate ceasefire reopening the Strait of Hormuz with 15-20 days for a broader settlement.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026.
Tehran's Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the former Supreme Leader, as the new Supreme Leader.
Iran named Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy commander, as new Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed last week.
U.S. President Donald Trump announced productive phone talks with a top Iranian official led by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.
Trump instructed the Pentagon to postpone attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days ending Friday.
Trump claimed Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz as a negotiation sign.
Pakistan delivered a U.S. 15-point peace plan to Iran covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran is reviewing the peace plan but rejects negotiations.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied that talks with the U.S. occurred.
Iran's foreign ministry noted messages from friendly states including Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey.
Trump warned of resumed bombing if diplomatic efforts fail.
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement demanding adversaries' security be removed.
Trump threatened strikes on Iranian power plants and energy sites, potentially causing widespread economic disruption.
Over 2,000 additional U.S. Marines are deploying to the Middle East by Friday.
Armed forces spokesperson General Abolfazl Shekarchi warned adversaries are unsafe in global 'tourist centers.'
Israeli strikes knocked out Tehran electricity infrastructure.
Iran retained functioning command and control and radar capabilities despite U.S. and Israeli strikes.
U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-SC) opposed troop deployments to the Middle East following a briefing.
Israel accelerated strikes ahead of potential war end.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 40 shows intensified regional attacks (79 strikes on UAE, missile/drone attacks on Jordan) amid contradictory diplomatic signals, with Pakistan-brokered 'Islamabad Accords' ceasefire proposal and a 5-day US bombing pause countered by Iran's hardline new leadership rejecting negotiations while maintaining operational capabilities.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Tehran's Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei as new Supreme Leader and named hardline Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council chief, consolidating hardline control as Pakistan delivers 15-point US peace plan including Strait of Hormuz reopening and nuclear rollback.
